title: Genetic transformation of Aureobasidium pullulans via Agrobacterium tumefaciens-mediated transformation
author: Julian Liber

steps.title: Transform binary plasmids capable of replicating in A
steps.instruction: Transform binary plasmids capable of replicating in A. tumefaciens and expressing in A. pullulans into A. tumefaciens AGL1 via electroporation. This vector on Addgene is a good starting point. See this protocol: https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.g8ubzww

steps.title: Modified from the cited protocol
steps.instruction: Modified from the cited protocol: without diluting, plate 20 µL of the resuspended cells on appropriate selective medium. Tilt the plate to create a drip, then incubate for 48:00:00 at 28 °C .

steps.title: Inoculate 5 mL of LM+ 15...
steps.instruction: Inoculate 5 mL of LM+ 15 ug/mL Rif with the appropriate antibiotic in a 50 mL tube with a streak of colonies taken from the electroporation-transformed cells.

steps.title: Inoculate 5 mL of YPD in...
steps.instruction: Inoculate 5 mL of YPD in a 50 mL tube with the yeast line intended for transformation.

steps.title: Pellet Agro cultures in tubes, by...
steps.instruction: Pellet Agro cultures in tubes, by centrifuging at 3000 x g, °C, for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Pour off or aspirate the supernatant,...
steps.instruction: Pour off or aspirate the supernatant, and replace with 5 mL of 1x IM. Vortex to resuspend.

steps.title: Replace into the incubator at 230...
steps.instruction: Replace into the incubator at 230 rpm, 28 °C, , for 4-6 hr.

steps.title: When Agrobacterium cells are nearly ready, centrifuge 1
steps.instruction: When Agrobacterium cells are nearly ready, centrifuge 1.5-2 mL of yeast culture in a microcentrifuge tube at 15000 x g, °C, 30 seconds . Discard supernatant, resuspend in 1 mL water, centrifuge again. Discard supernatant and suspend in 300 µL water (you will need 50 µL of 0.25 OD600 suspension per transformation).

steps.title: Measure the OD600 of the yeast suspensions
steps.instruction: Measure the OD600 of the yeast suspensions. Prepare dilutions at OD600 = 0.25 in water.

steps.title: Flame the glass test tube, and...
steps.instruction: Flame the glass test tube, and allow to cool in a sterile environment. Use it to press 4 divots in the IM plates, distributed in quadrants, as below.

steps.title: Mix 50 µL of yeast with...
steps.instruction: Mix 50 µL of yeast with 50 µL of induced Agrobacterium culture, then transfer all of the mixture to a divot in the plate.

steps.title: Allow plates to dry overnight on...
steps.instruction: Allow plates to dry overnight on the bench with lids on, then transfer to a 24C incubator in a bag or wrapped in parafilm. Incubate for 3+ days (ideally 5) until a lawn of colonies is present in the divot.

steps.title: Using a wood streaker, collect as...
steps.instruction: Using a wood streaker, collect as much of the co-culture in the divot as possible. Transfer to one quadrant of a square YPD plate with antibiotics as follows: Make a line at one edge of the area with all the cells. Streak back and forth over the area, moving gradually away from the line, to create a dilution from one side to the other, as below.

steps.title: Incubate the plate at 28 °C...
steps.instruction: Incubate the plate at 28 °C until colonies are visible and can be selected by fluorescence. Transfer the colonies to a new plate before genotyping by PCR because holdover bacteria can create false positives.
